title: IDEA INCUBATOR
blurb: Great businesses start by fixing something annoying. Today you'll hatch an idea people actually want!
mission: Learn to find and test a business idea that solves a real problem.
what: A good idea is one that solves a real problem for real people.
key: If it fixes a problem, people will pay for it.
swapGood: “Kids lose pencils, so I'll sell cute pencil clips.”
intro: Turn a problem into a plan…
rule: A real idea fixes a real problem.
goal: “Ask 5 people if they'd buy my idea before I build it.”
smartTip: A tested idea beats a lucky guess!
gbBody: Some ideas fix real needs; others are just random guesses.
tip: Ask people before you spend a dime.
badWarn: Random ideas waste your time and money!
goodNote: A tested idea is ready to grow!

title: BRANDING DISTRICT
blurb: A brand is how people remember you. Today you'll build a name and look folks won't forget!
mission: Learn to build a brand: a name, a logo, and what you stand for.
what: A brand is the name, look, and promise people remember you by.
key: A clear brand makes people trust and pick you.
swapGood: “I'm Sunny Slime — bright, fun, and never sticky!”
rule: Keep your brand simple and easy to remember.
goal: “Build one name, one logo, and one promise for my shop.”
smartTip: The same look every time builds trust!
gbBody: Some brands are clear and trusted; others confuse customers.
tip: Use the same name and colors everywhere.
badWarn: A confusing brand makes people forget you!
goodNote: A clear brand earns loyal fans!

title: PRODUCT LAB
blurb: Your first try is never the best try. Today you'll build, test, and improve until people love it!
mission: Learn to build a product and make it better with feedback.
what: A product is the thing or service you make for people to buy.
key: Test it, fix it, and it gets better every time.
swapGood: “They said it's too sweet, so I'll add less sugar.”
intro: Make it, then make it better…
star: Great products are improved, not perfect at first.
goal: “Make 3 versions and let friends pick the best one.”
smartTip: Small fixes add up to a great product!
gbBody: Some makers keep improving; others get stuck on version 1.
tip: Ask one tester what to fix next.
badWarn: A product that never improves gets left behind!

title: STARTUP STREET
blurb: A business needs you every day. Today you'll learn to run the daily jobs that keep money coming in!
mission: Learn to run daily operations and manage a small business.
what: Operations are the daily jobs that keep your business running.
key: Do the small jobs every day and your business stays open.
swapGood: “Every morning I check supplies and count my cash.”
rule: Do the daily jobs before they pile up.
star: Small daily habits keep a business alive.
goal: “Finish my 5 daily jobs before I close the shop.”
gbBody: Some owners run a tidy day; others let jobs pile up.
gbKey: Stay on top of daily jobs.
tip: Make tomorrow's list before you close.
badWarn: Skipped jobs pile up and lose you money!
goodNote: A steady routine keeps customers coming!

title: PITCH ARENA
blurb: Need cash to grow? You'll pitch your idea so investors say yes and hand you the funding!
mission: Learn to pitch your idea clearly to get funding.
what: A pitch is a short, clear talk that asks people to fund your idea.
key: Explain the problem, your fix, and what you need.
swapGood: “I need $50 to buy supplies and will pay it back in a month.”
rule: Be clear about the problem and your ask.
star: A clear pitch turns a maybe into a yes.
goal: “Ask for $50 and show how I'll turn it into $80.”
gbBody: Some pitches are clear and confident; others ramble and lose people.
tip: Practice your pitch in under one minute.
badWarn: A fuzzy pitch makes investors say no!
goodNote: A sharp pitch wins the funding!

title: MARKETING METRO
blurb: The best product needs customers. Today you'll spread the word and grow a crowd that loves you!
mission: Learn to get customers and grow your audience.
what: Marketing is how you tell people about what you sell.
key: The right message reaches the right buyers.
swapGood: “I'll make posters and tell every kid on my street.”
rule: Tell the right people the right way.
star: The right message brings the right fans.
goal: “Tell 20 people and turn them into 8 buyers.”
gbBody: Some sellers spread the word; others stay silent and wait.
tip: Ask one happy buyer to tell a friend.
badWarn: If nobody hears about you, nobody buys!
goodNote: Spreading the word grows your crowd fast!

title: SCALING CENTER
blurb: One shop is great, but why stop there? Today you'll learn to grow bigger and let smart systems help!
mission: Learn to expand and automate to grow a bigger business.
what: Scaling is growing your business to serve many more customers.
key: Smart systems let you grow without doing it all alone.
swapGood: “I'll make a recipe card so a helper can bake too.”
rule: Build systems so growth doesn't break you.
goal: “Add one helper and a system to double my sales.”
smartTip: Good systems let you grow while you sleep!
gbBody: Some owners build systems to grow; others stall doing it all alone.
tip: Write down one job so someone else can do it.
badWarn: Doing everything alone keeps you small!
goodNote: Smart systems turn one shop into many!
